jokes.text: What's the special occasion? / I decided to eat one.
jokes.setup: Someone asks about a special occasion for eating hot dogs
jokes.punchline: The circular logic that deciding to eat one IS the special occasion
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurdist logic delivered deadpan. Economy is excellent - maximum funny per word.

jokes.text: Church on a Tuesday? But he seemed so normal.
jokes.setup: Character goes to church on an unusual day
jokes.punchline: The assumption that religious behavior equals abnormality
jokes.explanation: Good character-based humor showing Liz's secular perspective

jokes.text: Or your job, Jack. Or his job!
jokes.setup: Jack jokingly suggests Kenneth might take Devin's job
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's innocent escalation suggesting he could take everyone's jobs
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth - his innocent ambition is both endearing and absurd

jokes.text: You saw me leave the apartment this morning. / I saw you put it on and I thought it looked nice.
jokes.setup: Two people wearing the same outfit
jokes.punchline: Pete's genuine compliment revealing their uncomfortable living situation
jokes.explanation: Awkward domestic situation humor with good character specificity

jokes.text: How long are you staying with me? / Indefinitely.
jokes.setup: Liz asking about Pete's stay duration
jokes.punchline: Pete's matter-of-fact indefinite timeline
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective - the casual delivery of terrible news

jokes.text: Wow, if this turns into a showdown, you guys could settle it with a talking like this contest.
jokes.setup: Jack and Devin's corporate rivalry
jokes.punchline: Liz mocking their identical posh speaking patterns
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about corporate speech patterns that's both specific and universal

jokes.text: He pioneered the concept of ten second internet sitcoms. Making it happen / Honey, I'm home. Oh, great. We made it
jokes.setup: Jack describing Devin's innovative web content
jokes.punchline: The ridiculously compressed sitcom format demonstration
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of internet content and compression of traditional formats

jokes.text: They can do shapes now. One time, I saw a cowboy hat. Boom, boom, boom.
jokes.setup: Jack pitching fireworks as sophisticated entertainment
jokes.punchline: His childlike wonder at basic firework shapes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack Donaghy - mixing corporate speak with genuine boyish enthusiasm

jokes.text: Those weren't jokes. That was an appeal for a return to common sense and decency. / Well, it got big laughs.
jokes.setup: Jack complimenting Liz's political humor
jokes.punchline: Liz's serious political writing accidentally being funny
jokes.explanation: Great character comedy - Liz's earnestness being funnier than intended humor

jokes.text: Lamazewell.
jokes.setup: Writers brainstorming about alien abductions in Roswell
jokes.punchline: Combines 'Lamaze' and 'Roswell' into terrible portmanteau
jokes.explanation: Weak pun that demonstrates bad comedy writing - intentionally bad

jokes.text: I never got out of my car and she never got all the way out of her tollbooth!
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining why he couldn't be the father
jokes.punchline: The bizarre logistics of tollbooth romance
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant absurdist image that's both ridiculous and somehow believable for Tracy

jokes.text: What is this, 1992?
jokes.setup: Suggestion for Bill Clinton eating hamburgers sketch
jokes.punchline: Calling out dated comedy references
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on comedy writing but fairly standard

jokes.text: I'm not a comedy writer.
jokes.setup: Being criticized for bad comedy ideas
jokes.punchline: Admitting he's not actually a comedy writer
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware moment explaining the terrible pitches

jokes.text: So what team do you play for? / Oh, it's not really a team. It's just a bunch of guys who like doing gymnastics.
jokes.setup: Devin asking about Kenneth's sexual orientation euphemistically
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's literal interpretation about actual gymnastics
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth innocence meeting adult themes - classic misdirection

jokes.text: Good God. Devin is gay. He's even more powerful than I thought.
jokes.setup: Jack learning about Devin's sexuality
jokes.punchline: Jack treating gayness as a corporate superpower
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack logic - viewing everything through corporate power dynamics

jokes.text: Science is whatever we want it to be.
jokes.setup: Tracy asking about DNA test capabilities
jokes.punchline: Dr. Spaceman's complete misunderstanding of scientific method
jokes.explanation: Perfectly encapsulates Dr. Spaceman's quackery in one brilliant line

jokes.text: Boy, it's crazy to think we used to settle questions of paternity by dunking a woman in water until she admitted she made it all up.
jokes.setup: Discussing modern paternity testing
jokes.punchline: Dr. Spaceman's casual reference to medieval torture as paternity testing
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered with Dr. Spaceman's characteristic casual insanity

jokes.text: Different time, the '60s.
jokes.setup: Talking about medieval-style paternity testing
jokes.punchline: Dr. Spaceman placing medieval practices in the 1960s
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dr. Spaceman - completely wrong historical timeline delivered with confidence

jokes.text: Love is like an onion, and you peel away layer after stinky layer until you're just... weeping over the sink.
jokes.setup: Discussion about revealing personal information in relationships
jokes.punchline: Pete's cynical extended metaphor about love
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of romantic metaphors with perfectly crafted imagery

jokes.text: I call that one 'Art School.' And one about a Jewish guy who opens an ice cream parlor. That one's called 'Ice Cream Cohen.'
jokes.setup: Devin pitching TV show ideas
jokes.punchline: The terrible pun-based show titles
jokes.explanation: Deliberately bad puns that work because they're satirizing terrible TV pitches

jokes.text: I'll go slip into something a little more comfortable. Like in the movies!
jokes.setup: Devin using a seductive line
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's innocent movie reference interpretation
jokes.explanation: Kenneth's innocence playing perfectly against adult seduction attempts

jokes.text: Oh my. That's an awful short robe. / I know. I had to cut it down myself.
jokes.setup: Kenneth commenting on Devin's robe
jokes.punchline: Devin's matter-of-fact explanation of his DIY seduction outfit
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy enhanced by the matter-of-fact delivery

jokes.text: Can you give me a hand, Kenneth? / For a fellow page, anything!
jokes.setup: Devin asking for help with clear double entendre
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's enthusiastic innocent interpretation
jokes.explanation: Kenneth's page loyalty trumping any other interpretation is perfectly in character

jokes.text: I'm gonna have to ask at all four of us keep this to ourselves.
jokes.setup: Dr. Spaceman discussing doctor-patient confidentiality
jokes.punchline: His inability to count the people present
jokes.explanation: Classic Dr. Spaceman incompetence - can't even count basic numbers

jokes.text: Jasper Buceman? / No, Tracy. Our third president. Thomas Jefferson.
jokes.setup: Tracy learning he's related to the third president
jokes.punchline: Tracy's completely wrong guess about who that is
jokes.explanation: Tracy's historical ignorance creating a perfectly absurd moment

jokes.text: Yeah, that guy was into black chicks. I'm surprised I am not a descendant.
jokes.setup: Learning about Jefferson's relationships with slaves
jokes.punchline: Toofer's matter-of-fact acknowledgment of historical sexual exploitation
jokes.explanation: Dark historical humor delivered with Toofer's characteristic bluntness

jokes.text: This is how black people dial a phone. *Boop ood a-boop a-doop a-doop whoop* This is how white people dial a phone.
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining his comedy is based on racial differences
jokes.punchline: The absurd demonstration of racial phone-dialing stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy that satirizes both racial stereotypes and Tracy's comedy style

jokes.text: I'm straighter than you are gay and I leave particles of guys like you in my wind.
jokes.setup: Jack responding to Devin's challenge
jokes.punchline: Jack's bizarre metaphor about defeating corporate rivals
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Jack - mixing sexuality and corporate dominance in one absurd metaphor

jokes.text: No, Devin. I don't do that.
jokes.setup: Devin saying 'You're going down'
jokes.punchline: Jack's literal interpretation of the sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and delivery - Jack's straight-faced rejection of the implication

jokes.text: Fine. But I will be reporting this to the Fox Problem Solvers.
jokes.setup: Liz being told not to eat so many hot dogs
jokes.punchline: Her threat to report this minor interaction to a consumer advocacy show
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz escalation - turning minor criticism into a consumer rights issue

jokes.text: Hi, my name is Floyd and I'm an alcoholic.
jokes.setup: Liz following Floyd to what she thinks is church
jokes.punchline: Discovering it's actually an AA meeting
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection that completely reframes the situation and Liz's assumptions

jokes.text: Hi, my name is Floyd and I'm an alcoholic. Hi, Floyd.
jokes.setup: Liz discovers Floyd at what she thought was church
jokes.punchline: It's actually an AA meeting with standard group response
jokes.explanation: Good setup/revelation moment that recontextualizes earlier church mystery

jokes.text: One day at a time, Pete. I'm gonna take it one day at a time.
jokes.setup: Pete asking about Liz's plan for fake alcoholism
jokes.punchline: Liz using AA language to describe her deception
jokes.explanation: Brilliant ironic use of recovery language to describe continued deception

jokes.text: I had to keep talking just to stop him from putting his fingers in my mouth.
jokes.setup: Jack asking why Kenneth revealed information to Devin
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's innocent description of Devin's seduction attempt
jokes.explanation: Kenneth's matter-of-fact delivery of something so inappropriate is perfectly uncomfortable

jokes.text: Kenneth, you are the worst gay bait ever.
jokes.setup: Kenneth's failed seduction mission
jokes.punchline: Jack's blunt assessment of Kenneth's espionage skills
jokes.explanation: Jack's corporate disappointment in Kenneth's seduction abilities is perfectly absurd

jokes.text: Like on What's Happening when that man used Rerun to bootleg that Doobie Brothers concert!
jokes.setup: Kenneth understanding he was used
jokes.punchline: His incredibly specific pop culture reference to explain the situation
jokes.explanation: Kenneth's encyclopedic TV knowledge producing the perfect analogy for his situation

jokes.text: Just like Sydney Bristow on Alias, I'll use my sexuality as a weapon.
jokes.setup: Kenneth agreeing to distract Devin again
jokes.punchline: Kenneth comparing himself to a spy show femme fatale
jokes.explanation: Absolutely perfect Kenneth - his innocent confidence in his own seductive abilities

jokes.text: Maybe it was some sort of brace or corrective boot you wore during childhood.
jokes.setup: Jack commenting on Liz's sense of humor
jokes.punchline: His theory that childhood medical trauma created her comedy
jokes.explanation: Jack's psychological analysis being both specific and completely inappropriate

jokes.text: It's like the audience for a Bobby McFerrin concert up in here!
jokes.setup: Tracy describing his racial identity crisis
jokes.punchline: The specific reference to Bobby McFerrin's diverse audience
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific cultural reference that perfectly captures racial mixing

jokes.text: I think he's shaking hands with Robert E. Lee.
jokes.setup: Toofer showing his proud family Civil War photo
jokes.punchline: Tracy identifying the Confederate general instead of Union
jokes.explanation: Devastating historical revelation that completely undermines Toofer's family pride

jokes.text: Arbor Day, the Boston Marathon and Hitler's birthday.
jokes.setup: Listing remaining April holidays for Jack's special
jokes.punchline: The casual inclusion of Hitler's birthday with benign events
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation that works through the casual delivery of horrible information

jokes.text: America, which I invented...
jokes.setup: Thomas Jefferson giving Tracy advice
jokes.punchline: Jefferson claiming to have invented America
jokes.explanation: Absurd historical claim that fits the dream logic of the sequence

jokes.text: Well, I only have Star Wars and Tootsie, so we just keeping watching those two over and over.
jokes.setup: Liz describing her AFI top 100 movie project
jokes.punchline: Her limited collection forcing endless repetition
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz - starting an ambitious project without proper preparation

jokes.text: Right, but isn't that one of those rules like, 'don't walk between the subway cars,' and all the cool people just do it anyway?
jokes.setup: Floyd explaining AA dating rules
jokes.punchline: Liz's attempt to rationalize rule-breaking with subway analogy
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Liz's attempts to justify breaking rules

jokes.text: I ended up doing a man-on-the street commercial for Tarzan on Ice.
jokes.setup: Floyd describing his last drinking incident
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous specificity of his drunk commercial appearance
jokes.explanation: Absurd specific detail that perfectly captures drunk poor decision-making

jokes.text: You warlock! You came to entrance me. Donaghy sent you.
jokes.setup: Kenneth's seduction attempt being exposed
jokes.punchline: Devin calling Kenneth a 'warlock' and realizing the setup
jokes.explanation: The term 'warlock' for Kenneth's seduction powers is perfectly absurd

jokes.text: Walk it off, scarecrow.
jokes.setup: Liz having circulation problems from sleeping position
jokes.punchline: Floyd's affectionate but accurate physical description
jokes.explanation: Gentle physical comedy with an endearing nickname

jokes.text: I know it makes me seem just nut-log Ann Heche crazy
jokes.setup: Liz confessing her deception to Floyd
jokes.punchline: Her specific reference to Ann Heche as the standard for crazy behavior
jokes.explanation: Dated pop culture reference that fits Liz's specific cultural knowledge

jokes.text: I feel more confused and betrayed than those people that worked with Tootsie.
jokes.setup: Floyd expressing his feelings about Liz's deception
jokes.punchline: Comparing his situation to the characters in the movie they just watched
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that connects the movie plot to the real situation perfectly

jokes.text: MC Lyte just murdered Danny Bonaduce? Oh, thanks, phone!
jokes.setup: Devin pitching celebrity death content for phones
jokes.punchline: The casual announcement of celebrity murder via mobile device
jokes.explanation: Dark absurdist content that satirizes both reality TV and mobile entertainment

jokes.text: The kinds of shows we watched as kids sitting on our neighbor's knee.
jokes.setup: Jack describing nostalgic television experiences
jokes.punchline: The uncomfortable implication of the neighbor's knee reference
jokes.explanation: Subtle dark humor that works through implication rather than explicit statement

jokes.text: Confederate Monster: The Tobias Spurlock Disaster.
jokes.setup: Tracy finding a biography about Toofer's ancestor
jokes.punchline: The dramatically terrible title revealing more family shame
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of Toofer's family shame with a dramatically awful book title

jokes.text: It's not a comedy. It's a drama.
jokes.setup: Tracy describing his Thomas Jefferson movie idea
jokes.punchline: His insistence that playing all the parts isn't meant to be funny
jokes.explanation: Tracy's complete lack of self-awareness about his own absurdity

jokes.text: It's the story of Thomas Jefferson, only thing we're gonna do it like Norbit, where I play all the parts. That's actually hilarious. It's not a comedy. It's a drama.
jokes.setup: Tracy pitches Jefferson movie like Norbit
jokes.punchline: Insists the inherently comedic concept is serious drama
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy delusion - completely absurd concept presented with total seriousness

jokes.text: I have had three donuts so far today. A couple months of ago I went on a date with my cousin.
jokes.setup: Liz confessing personal secrets to even things out
jokes.punchline: The casual inclusion of cousin-dating among mundane confessions
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation mixing mundane and shocking confessions

jokes.text: Uh, once in college, I pooped my pants a little bit at a Country Steaks all-you-can-eat buffet, and I didn't leave until I finished my second plate of shrimp.
jokes.setup: Liz continues confessing embarrassing secrets
jokes.punchline: Admits to staying at buffet despite bowel accident to finish eating
jokes.explanation: Gross but perfectly Liz confession showing her food priorities over dignity

jokes.text: I have some weird sexual fantasy stuff about Gopher from The Love Boat.
jokes.setup: Liz's childhood Love Boat ritual
jokes.punchline: The resulting sexual fixation on a minor TV character
jokes.explanation: Perfectly specific and embarrassing confession that feels authentically Liz

jokes.text: I have had five donuts today.
jokes.setup: Liz previously confessing to three donuts
jokes.punchline: Revealing she lied about even that mundane detail
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback showing Liz can't stop lying even during her honesty confession

jokes.text: Except mine's the one from Caddyshack. Well, that makes sense 'cause he's a very good dancer.
jokes.setup: Floyd revealing his own Gopher fantasy
jokes.punchline: Liz's matter-of-fact analysis of the dancing skills
jokes.explanation: Sweet moment of connection over shared weird attraction with logical justification

jokes.text: Wait, fireworks? In Midtown? On a day that is not the Fourth of July? Oh, my God.
jokes.setup: Liz realizing Jack's fireworks show is happening
jokes.punchline: Her dawning horror at the implications for NYC
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing the real-world consequences of Jack's corporate scheming
